seo.title: Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents in LangChain Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix sensitive data leakage prevention in ai agents in LangChain agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In LangChain agents, sensitive data leakage often occurs when agents inadvertently expose API keys, user PII, or internal secrets in tool calls, chain outputs, or hallucinated responses due to unfiltered LLM generations. This is exacerbated by LangChain's flexible agent executors and retrieval chains, where intermediate states like ReAct agent thoughts or RAG context can propagate sensitive data without sanitization. Without proper guards, LangChain applications become prime targets for data exfiltration attacks.

howItManifests.scenario: ReAct Agent Tool Calls with Embedded Secrets
howItManifests.symptom: Agent includes API keys from user input in tool arguments
howItManifests.impact: Attackers extract credentials from agent executor traces

howItManifests.symptom: Retrieved documents containing PII appear in final chain output
howItManifests.impact: Customer data exposed in chatbot responses

howItManifests.symptom: ChatHistory buffer retains sensitive messages across sessions
howItManifests.impact: Historical PII accessible to unauthorized users

howItManifests.symptom: Tool input parameters with secrets logged to LangSmith traces
howItManifests.impact: Secrets visible in LangSmith observability dashboard

detection.name: Agent Output PII Scanner
detection.description: Detects common PII patterns and API key formats in agent final outputs
detection.code:
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor
from langchain.tools import Tool
import re

PII_PATTERNS = [
    r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b',
    r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b',
    r'(sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{48})',
    r'(ak-[A-Za-z0-9]{20})'
]

def scan_for_pii(text: str) -> list:
    matches = []
    for pattern in PII_PATTERNS:
        found = re.finditer(pattern, text)
        matches.extend([m.group() for m in found])
    return matches

# Usage in agent executor
result = agent_executor.invoke({"input": user_query})
leaks = scan_for_pii(result['output'])
if leaks:
    print(f'🚨 PII Leak detected: {leaks}')

detection.name: Tool Call Argument Inspector
detection.description: Intercepts agent tool calls to scan arguments for sensitive data before execution

detection.name: LangSmith Trace PII Monitor
detection.description: Scans LangSmith run traces for sensitive data exposure
detection.code:
from langsmith import Client
import re

client = Client()

runs = client.list_runs(project_name="my-agent")
for run in runs:
    if run.outputs:
        output_text = str(run.outputs)
        # Check for common secret patterns
        if re.search(r'(sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{48}|Bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9]{40})', output_text):
            print(f'🚨 Leak in run {run.id}: {output_text[:100]}...')
            client.update_run(run.id, {'extra': {'security_alert': True}})

fixes.name: Output Sanitization Chain Wrapper
fixes.description: Wraps agent outputs with regex-based PII redaction before returning to user
fixes.code:
import re
from typing import Dict, Any
from langchain.schema import BaseOutputParser

class SecureOutputParser(BaseOutputParser):
    def parse(self, text: str) -> str:
        # Redact PII patterns
        text = re.sub(r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b', '[EMAIL_REDACTED]', text)
        text = re.sub(r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b', '[SSN_REDACTED]', text)
        text = re.sub(r'sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{48}', '[API_KEY_REDACTED]', text)
        return text

# Apply to agent
agent_with_security = agent | SecureOutputParser()
result = agent_with_security.invoke({"input": user_input})

fixes.name: Secure Tool Input Validator
fixes.description: Validates and sanitizes tool call arguments before execution
fixes.code:
from langchain.tools.base import BaseTool

class SecureTool(BaseTool):
    name = "secure_calculator"
    description = "Secure calculator tool"
    
    def _run(self, expression: str):
        # Sanitize input
        if any(keyword in expression.lower() for keyword in ['api_key', 'password', 'secret']):
            raise ValueError('Invalid input contains sensitive keywords')
        return eval(expression)  # Simplified - use safe eval in production

# Replace regular tools with secure versions
tools = [SecureTool()]

fixes.name: Memory Buffer Sanitizer
fixes.description: Automatically sanitizes chat history before adding to memory buffer

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade sensitive data leakage prevention by automatically wrapping LangChain chain.invoke() and agent_executor.invoke() calls with runtime verification. Using predefined security detectors for PII, API keys, and secret patterns, Vex scans agent thoughts, tool calls, intermediate chain states, and final outputs in real-time. When leakage is detected, Vex can automatically redact sensitive data, block malicious requests, or trigger custom remediation workflows.

The platform integrates seamlessly with LangChain's callback system and LangSmith tracing, providing comprehensive coverage across RAG chains, conversational agents, and multi-step reasoning workflows. Vex's ML-powered detectors adapt to your specific sensitive data patterns while maintaining <1ms inference overhead. Teams can configure custom redaction rules, set leakage thresholds, and receive instant alerts through Slack, PagerDuty, or custom webhooks.

Vex also provides automated remediation through dynamic output patching, secure tool argument filtering, and memory sanitization, ensuring zero-trust security for LangChain applications without code changes.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import verify
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

# Vex automatically wraps all invoke calls
@verify(
    detectors=["pii", "api_keys", "secrets"],
    remediation="redact",
    alert_on="block"
)
def secure_agent_query(user_input: str):
    result = agent_executor.invoke({"input": user_input})
    return result['output']

# Single line integration for existing chains
result = verify(
    agent_executor.invoke,
    detectors=["pii", "financial_data"],
    input={"input": user_query}
)['output']

# Vex auto-instruments LangSmith callbacks
agent_executor = AgentExecutor(
    agent=agent,
    tools=tools,
    callbacks=[VexLangSmithCallback()]
)
